tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: CCBot is a Telegram bot that bridges Telegram Forum topics to Claude Code sessions via tmux windows. Each topic is bound to a tmux window running a Claude Code instance. Start with the README to understand the project's purpose, architecture, and setup instructions.

tour.title: Application Entry Point & Configuration
tour.description: The bot starts in main.py which sets up logging, loads configuration via the Config class, creates the bot application with rate limiting, and runs polling. The Config class reads environment variables for tokens, paths, and settings.

tour.title: Core Bot Application
tour.description: bot.py is the central orchestrator. It registers all handlers, manages user authorization, creates tmux windows for new topics, handles incoming messages/text/voice/photos, and dispatches callback queries. This is where the main message routing logic lives.

tour.title: Session & State Management
tour.description: SessionManager tracks thread-to-window bindings and persists state to disk. MonitorState manages read offsets for tailing session output. The SessionMonitor polls Claude Code JSONL logs to detect new messages and notifications. Utils provides atomic file I/O and directory resolution.

tour.title: Terminal & Tmux Integration
tour.description: TmuxManager wraps libtmux to create/manage/kill tmux windows. TerminalParser extracts structured content (interactive UIs, status lines, bash output, usage info) from raw terminal dumps. TranscriptParser parses Claude Code JSONL entries into structured message objects.

tour.title: Message Processing Pipeline
tour.description: Messages flow through a queue system (FIFO, with merging for tool_use/tool_result pairs). MessageSender handles Telegram API calls with MarkdownV2 formatting. MarkdownV2 converts and escapes markdown for Telegram. ResponseBuilder constructs message parts from parsed transcript entries.

tour.title: Interactive UI & Handlers
tour.description: Interactive UI handlers manage AskUserQuestion, ExitPlanMode, and Permission prompts via inline keyboards. DirectoryBrowser lets users pick working directories. History provides paginated message history. The hook module auto-installs SessionStart hooks into Claude Code settings.

tour.title: Testing Infrastructure
tour.description: Tests are organized under tests/ with unit tests for each module and integration tests for config and monitor_state. Test fixtures provide sample terminal output, JSONL entries, and mock bots for testing handlers.

project.name: ccbot
project.description: Telegram Bot for monitoring Claude Code sessions
